title: 3-HYDROXY-1-p-SULPHONATOPHENYL-3-PHENYTLTRIAZINE AS AN INDIRECT SPECTROPHOTOMETRIC REAGENT FOR FLUORIDE
author: MISS SUSHILA MEHRA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The fading of ferric-3-hydroxy-l-p-sulphonatophenyl-3-phenyltriazine, a bluish black complex with fluoride ions, is made the basis for the indirect spectrophotometric determination of fluoride in the concentration range of 0.5 to 40 p.p.m. Phosphate, borate, citrate, tartrate, oxalate, and cyanide interfere; other common anions do not. This method can be applied for determining fluoride in water samples.
steps_raw: The fading of ferric-3-hydroxy-l-p-sulphonatophenyl-3-phenyltriazine, a bluish black complex with fluoride ions, is made the basis for the indirect spectrophotometric determination of fluoride in the concentration range of 0.5 to 40 p.p.m. Phosphate, borate, citrate, tartrate, oxalate, and cyanide interfere; other common anions do not. This method can be applied for determining fluoride in water samples.
